content: Adapting the contextual retrieval technique from retrieval-augmented agent context management to streaming experience processing for Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) training can dynamically pull only the most relevant, recently encountered novel experiences into ICM's forward and inverse reward model updates, rather than processing every incoming streaming experience indiscriminately. This creates a curiosity-driven agent that prioritizes updating its reward model on unexpected, high-information experiences instead of wasting compute on redundant or familiar observations from the stream.
source_topics: Streaming experience processing for ICM training
